name: Monorepo with npm Workspaces
description: Set up and manage a monorepo using npm workspaces for shared packages, consistent tooling, and streamlined development.

steps.title: Initialize the monorepo root
steps.description: Create the root package.json with workspaces configuration pointing to your package directories.
steps.command: npm init -y && npm pkg set workspaces='["packages/*", "apps/*"]' && npm pkg set private=true
steps.tip: Always set private: true on the root package.json to prevent accidentally publishing the monorepo itself.

steps.title: Create workspace packages
steps.description: Set up individual packages within the monorepo with their own package.json files.
steps.command:
mkdir -p packages/shared apps/web apps/api
npm init -y -w packages/shared
npm init -y -w apps/web
npm init -y -w apps/api

steps.title: Add cross-workspace dependencies
steps.description: Reference shared packages as dependencies in other workspaces using the workspace protocol.
steps.command:
npm pkg set dependencies.@myorg/shared="*" -w apps/web
npm pkg set dependencies.@myorg/shared="*" -w apps/api
npm install
steps.tip: npm automatically symlinks workspace packages. Changes to shared code are immediately available without publishing.

steps.title: Run scripts across workspaces
steps.description: Execute npm scripts in specific workspaces or across all workspaces from the root.
steps.command:
# Run in a specific workspace:
npm run build -w apps/web

# Run in all workspaces that have the script:
npm run build --workspaces --if-present

# Run tests everywhere:
npm test --workspaces --if-present

steps.title: Install dependencies to specific workspaces
steps.description: Add dependencies to individual workspace packages rather than the root.
steps.command:
npm install express -w apps/api
npm install react react-dom -w apps/web
npm install typescript -w packages/shared
steps.warning: Install dependencies where they are used, not at the root. Root devDependencies should only be shared tooling like ESLint or TypeScript.

steps.title: Create a shared TypeScript configuration
steps.description: Set up a shared tsconfig that all workspace packages extend for consistent TypeScript settings.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Node.js Monorepo Setup with npm Workspaces

ORG_NAME="${1:-myorg}"

echo "==> Initializing monorepo root..."
npm init -y
npm pkg set private=true
npm pkg set workspaces='["packages/*", "apps/*"]'
npm pkg set scripts.build="npm run build --workspaces --if-present"
npm pkg set scripts.test="npm run test --workspaces --if-present"
npm pkg set scripts.lint="npm run lint --workspaces --if-present"
npm pkg set scripts.clean="npm run clean --workspaces --if-present"

echo "==> Creating workspace directories..."
mkdir -p packages/shared/src apps/web/src apps/api/src

echo "==> Setting up shared package..."
cd packages/shared
npm init -y
npm pkg set name="@${ORG_NAME}/shared"
npm pkg set main="dist/index.js"
npm pkg set types="dist/index.d.ts"
npm pkg set scripts.build="tsc"
npm pkg set scripts.clean="rm -rf dist"

cat > src/index.ts <<'EOF'
export function formatDate(date: Date): string {
  return date.toISOString().split('T')[0];
}

export function slugify(text: string): string {
  return text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-').replace(/(^-|-$)/g, '');
}
EOF
cd ../..

echo "==> Setting up API app..."
cd apps/api
npm init -y
npm pkg set name="@${ORG_NAME}/api"
npm pkg set scripts.build="tsc"
npm pkg set scripts.start="node dist/index.js"
npm pkg set scripts.dev="node --watch src/index.ts"

cat > src/index.ts <<'EOF'
import { formatDate } from '@myorg/shared';
console.log('API started at', formatDate(new Date()));
EOF
cd ../..

echo "==> Setting up Web app..."
cd apps/web
npm init -y
npm pkg set name="@${ORG_NAME}/web"
npm pkg set scripts.build="echo 'build web'"
npm pkg set scripts.dev="echo 'dev web'"
cd ../..

echo "==> Installing dependencies..."
npm install typescript -w packages/shared
npm install @${ORG_NAME}/shared -w apps/api
npm install @${ORG_NAME}/shared -w apps/web
npm install

echo ""
echo "==> Monorepo structure:"
find . -name 'package.json' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' | sort

echo ""
echo "==> Commands:"
echo "  npm run build               # Build all workspaces"
echo "  npm run build -w apps/api   # Build specific workspace"
echo "  npm test --workspaces       # Test all workspaces"
echo "  npm install pkg -w apps/api # Add dep to specific workspace"

faq.question: Should I use npm workspaces, yarn workspaces, or pnpm?
faq.answer: npm workspaces work well for most projects and require no extra tooling. pnpm is faster and uses less disk space with its content-addressable store. Yarn is mature with good monorepo tooling. Choose based on your team's familiarity and project needs.

faq.question: How do I publish individual packages from a monorepo?
faq.answer: Run npm publish from the individual workspace directory, or use npm publish -w packages/shared from the root. For automated versioning and publishing, tools like changesets or lerna work well with npm workspaces.

faq.question: How do I handle different Node.js versions across workspaces?
faq.answer: Use the engines field in each workspace package.json to declare version requirements. Use a .nvmrc or .node-version at the root. All workspaces share the same Node.js runtime, so the root should use the strictest requirement.
